questions.question: Which of the following is a marine fish commonly consumed in India?
To establish a pure line in any animal species, which breeding strategy must be employed continuously?
Which product of apiculture is used extensively in cosmetics, polishes, and the pharmaceutical industry?
Which of the following lists contains only marine fishes?
The term 'Outbreeding' collectively refers to which of the following techniques?
During MOET, the fertilized eggs at 8-32 cell stages are transferred to surrogate mothers. What is the fundamental advantage of using surrogates instead of the genetic mother?

options.A: It allows the genetic mother to undergo another round of superovulation to yield more embryos
options.B: Surrogates have better lactation capacity than the genetic mother
options.C: It prevents the genetic mother from transmitting viral infections
options.D: Surrogates naturally produce more growth hormone ensuring larger calves

questions.question: A dairy farmer finds that their usually highly productive herd is showing signs of inbreeding depression. What is the immediate recommended step?

options.A: Mate the selected animals with an unrelated superior male of the same breed (Out-crossing)
options.B: Mate them with a male of a completely different breed (Cross-breeding)
options.C: Keep inbreeding but increase the concentrate in their feed

questions.question: Which of the following is the most significant disadvantage of continuous inbreeding over several generations?

options.A: Reduction in fertility and productivity (inbreeding depression)
options.C: Introduction of undesirable traits from foreign breeds
options.D: Increase in the size and weight of the progeny beyond manageable limits

questions.question: In poultry management, which of the following practices is essential to prevent the spread of devastating diseases like Ranikhet?

options.A: Maintaining strict hygiene, proper ventilation, and regular vaccination
options.B: Feeding the birds exclusively with high-protein oceanic fish
options.C: Keeping the birds isolated in completely dark environments
options.D: Inbreeding the birds continuously to develop natural resistance

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of storing semen in Artificial Insemination?

options.A: It allows the semen to be used long after the donor male is dead or transported far away.
options.B: It immediately cures sterility in the recipient female.
options.D: It guarantees that the resulting offspring will be exclusively female.

questions.question: What happens if continuous inbreeding is maintained over 5-6 generations in cattle?

options.A: Inbreeding depression occurs, causing reduced fertility and milk yield.
options.B: The herd becomes fully resistant to all diseases.
options.D: The offspring mutate into a new breed entirely.
